sourceMovie.title: Fast Times at Ridgemont High
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Fast Times at Ridgemont High works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Pie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fast Times at Ridgemont High clicked for you, American Pie (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fast Times at Ridgemont High?
faq.answer: Try American Pie, The DUFF, The Spectacular Now—each shares DNA with Fast Times at Ridgemont High while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fast Times at Ridgemont High?
faq.answer: Fast Times at Ridgemont High (1982) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fast Times at Ridgemont High worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
